summary: Phase 6 (Thawani + auto-simulate) verification COMPLETE. Backend: 12/13 passed, 1 skipped (Chatwoot upstream 502 on demo simulate — pre-existing, same as iteration_3). Frontend: all new behaviors verified end-to-end via Playwright — auto-simulate toggle with countdown, mock topup instant credit, ?topup=success and ?topup=cancelled URL param handling with URL cleanup, admin login regression. New /payments/config and /webhooks/thawani endpoints behave correctly in mock mode.

minor.issue: Returns 502 when Chatwoot upstream (https://letsm.io) is unavailable — known pre-existing issue tracked in iteration_3, NOT a regression. Wallet/auth/etc all unaffected.
When THAWANI_WEBHOOK_SECRET is empty, signature verification is bypassed (by design for mock mode). Make sure the env var is set before going live.

critical_code_review_comments: thawani.py: signature verification uses hmac.compare_digest correctly; returns False (not raises) on missing inputs — safe. Good.
server.py /webhooks/thawani: correctly idempotent — if txn already PAID, returns {already_paid: true} without double-crediting. Verified.

Wallet.jsx useEffect for URL params runs only on mount with empty deps + eslint-disable — works correctly but couples to window.location directly instead of react-router useSearchParams. Acceptable for MVP.
Channels.jsx WhatsAppConnectedCard auto-mode useEffect: when autoMode flips off via countdown completion, the interval `burst` is also cleaned up correctly via return cleanup. Good.

Auto-mode interval fires onSimulate() every 45s for 5 min — that's ~6-7 demo messages per session. If Chatwoot is rate-limited or down, errors will flood the UI. Consider catching errors silently in auto-mode (currently each failure sets error state via simulateMessage's try/catch — minor UX nit).
server.py is now ~1120 lines — well past the 700-line guideline. Strongly recommend extracting (a) payments/wallet routes, (b) WhatsApp routes, (c) admin routes into separate router modules.
TOPUP_PACKAGES, MESSAGE_PRICE_OMR, PLAN_PRICE, PLAN_CATALOG are module-level constants in server.py — would be cleaner in a config/billing.py module.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_phase6_thawani.py (NEW — 13 test cases covering /payments/config mock, /wallet/packages with gateway field, /me/wallet/topup mock-mode regression, /webhooks/thawani checkout.completed + payment.failed + already_paid idempotency, demo simulate 400 no_demo_channel, full connect→simulate flow, auth/me, admin overview, me/account regression)

success_rate.backend: 100% (12/12 executed, 1 skipped due to upstream Chatwoot 502 — not a regression)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (auto-simulate toggle, mock topup instant credit, ?topup success+cancelled URL handling, admin login regression — all verified)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@socialhub.om / Admin@2026. Client: ahmed@test.com / Test@1234 (company_name='متجر أحمد').
seed_data_creation: Used pre-existing ahmed@test.com. Re-connected demo WhatsApp channel via /api/whatsapp/connect (mock). Created one mock TOPUP txn (12.5 OMR) via the UI during regression — wallet balance went 100.00 → 112.50 (verified).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 6 verified clean. Thawani is in MOCK mode (env vars empty). To test real Thawani: set THAWANI_SECRET_KEY + THAWANI_PUBLISHABLE_KEY + THAWANI_WEBHOOK_SECRET in /app/backend/.env, then /api/payments/config will report active_gateway='thawani' and /api/me/wallet/topup will return payment_url. Webhook flow can be tested by POSTing checkout.completed with client_reference_id matching a PENDING txn ID. The demo simulate endpoint returns 502 when Chatwoot is down — unrelated to Phase 6 work. To run backend tests: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://storage-system-test.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_phase6_thawani.py -v`.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All Phase 6 features (auto-simulate UI, Thawani backend stubs, payments config endpoint, webhook handler, wallet topup_url redirect path) implemented correctly and behave as specified.
